info.name: How's Working There API
info.description: REST API for the How's Working There iOS app. All endpoints require Auth0 JWT Bearer token authentication. Base URL should be set to either local dev (http://localhost:8080) or production.

variable.description: API base URL - change to production when deploying
Auth0 JWT Bearer token - get via '0. Get Auth0 Token' request or tools/get-auth0-token.go
User ID (Auth0 sub claim) - must match the token's sub claim
Location UUID - set after creating a check-in
Check-in UUID - client-generated for idempotency (use uuidgen or similar)
Your Auth0 application Client ID (for token requests)

item.name: 0. Authentication

request.description:
Get an Auth0 access token using username/password (Resource Owner Password Flow).

Prerequisites:
1. Enable 'Password' grant type in Auth0 Application settings → Advanced → Grant Types
2. Create a test user in Auth0 Authentication → Database → Users
3. Set collection variables: auth0_domain, auth0_client_id, auth0_audience, auth0_email, auth0_password

After running this request, copy the 'access_token' from the response and paste it into the 'auth_token' collection variable.

Alternative: Use the CLI tool:
  go run tools/get-auth0-token.go -email user@example.com -password secret123

exec: console.log('✅ Token saved to auth_token variable');
console.log('❌ No access_token in response:', jsonData);

item.description:
Authentication helpers for getting Auth0 tokens.

## Quick Start

### Option 1: Postman Request (Recommended)
1. Run the 'Get Auth0 Token' request below
2. The token is automatically saved to the {{auth_token}} variable

### Option 2: Go CLI Tool
```bash
go run tools/get-auth0-token.go \
  -domain dev-xxxx.us.auth0.com \
  -client-id YOUR_CLIENT_ID \
  -audience https://api.howsworkingthere.com \
  -email test@example.com \
  -password secret123
```

### Option 3: Shell Script
```bash
export AUTH0_DOMAIN=dev-xxxx.us.auth0.com
export AUTH0_CLIENT_ID=YOUR_CLIENT_ID
export AUTH0_AUDIENCE=https://api.howsworkingthere.com
./tools/get-auth0-token.sh test@example.com secret123
```

## Required Variables

| Variable | Description | Where to Find |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `auth0_domain` | Your Auth0 tenant | Auth0 Dashboard → Applications → Settings → Domain |
| `auth0_client_id` | Application client ID | Auth0 Dashboard → Applications → Settings → Client ID |
| `auth0_audience` | API identifier | Auth0 Dashboard → APIs → Your API → Identifier |
| `auth0_email` | Test user email | Auth0 Dashboard → Authentication → Database → Users |
| `auth0_password` | Test user password | Same as above |

## Auth0 Setup Required

1. **Enable Password Grant**: Application → Advanced Settings → Grant Types → Check 'Password'
2. **Create Test User**: Authentication → Database → Create User
3. **Note on Social Logins**: Password flow only works for Database connections (email/password). For Apple/Google, use the Auth0 Dashboard's API section to generate test tokens.

query.description: Filter by location name (partial match, case-insensitive)
Center latitude for geo search (required with longitude)
Center longitude for geo search (required with latitude)

request.description: Search for existing locations by name, address, or geo-coordinates. Returns up to 50 matches. At least one search parameter is required. Uses Haversine formula for geo-distance calculation.

}

request.description: Create a full check-in with a new or existing location. This endpoint: (1) upserts the location (creates new or returns existing), (2) inserts check-in record, (3) inserts speed test, noise level, and workspace ratings. All operations are in a single database transaction. Client-generated `id` provides idempotency for retries.

Create a check-in at an existing location (use location_id from search or previous check-in). This skips the location upsert and directly associates the check-in, speed test, noise level, and ratings with the specified location. Returns 404 if location_id doesn't exist.

header.description: Auth0 JWT Bearer token - must match user_id

request.description: Get all locations the authenticated user has tested, with averaged scores from v_user_location_averages view. Results include: my_avg_download_mbps, my_avg_upload_mbps, my_avg_decibels, my_avg_crowdedness, etc. Returns 403 if JWT sub doesn't match the requested user_id.

query.description: Filter by location category (e.g., cafe, library, coworking)

request.description: Get all tested locations with aggregated scores from v_location_averages view. Results include community averages across all users: avg_download_mbps, avg_upload_mbps, avg_decibels, unique_users count, total_check_ins, etc. Supports filtering by category, geo-bounding, and pagination. Sorted by total_check_ins descending.
